design_philosophy.core_loop: Combat encounters mixed with environmental puzzle-solving and exploration, with continuous progression through interconnected chapter-based maps
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Survival and escape from Black Mesa facility while managing combat encounters against aliens, hostile soldiers, and environmental hazards
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying Gordon Freeman, a theoretical physicist navigating a catastrophic facility overrun by aliens, using environment and terrain strategically rather than pure firepower
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Valve's intentional rejection of cutscenes and shooting-gallery design in favor of continuous player control and scripted sequences that maintain first-person perspective; story told through environmental storytelling and NPC interactions rather than interruptions

difficulty.lives_system: Health-based damage system with HEV suit protection; no explicit lives or lives-per-attempt system
difficulty.continues_system: Checkpoint-based; player can reload from recent save progress
difficulty.checkpointing: Continuous progression through connected maps with implicit checkpointing at chapter transitions; no explicit mid-chapter checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player encounters varied enemy types and environmental hazards but must adapt tactics to terrain and available resources; boss encounters specifically designed to encourage environmental solutions rather than direct combat

progression.upgrade_system: HEV suit equipment upgrades including long jump module received late-game; weapon acquisition through exploration and enemy drops
progression.unlock_mechanism: Equipment unlocks through narrative progression (long jump module for endgame platforming); new areas accessed through puzzle-solving and combat advancement
replayability_hooks: Environmental puzzle variety encouraging different approaches
Mod support enabling community-created content and gameplay variations

world_building_techniques: Scripted sequences maintaining player control and first-person perspective
Environmental storytelling through facility design and NPC dialogue
Character voice lines from security guards and scientists conveying plot information
Minimal cutscene use (only one short cutscene reported); maintains immersion through continuous gameplay
Facility hierarchy and structure (from underground labs to surface to alien dimension Xen) reflects narrative progression

boss_design.philosophy: Terrain-based encounters rather than direct confrontation; bosses typically define chapters and require environmental manipulation or strategic positioning rather than pure firepower
notable_bosses: Giant creature in rocket engine test facility (defeated using environmental hazards)
Various alien organisms that define chapter boundaries

faction_indicators: Black Mesa Research Facility scientists (allied, provide assistance and information)
U.S. federal government soldiers/Marines (hostile, attempting cover-up)
Alien creatures from resonance cascade (hostile, primary antagonists)
danger_signals: Resonance cascade event causing facility breakdown and alien teleportation
Government military dispatch to eliminate witnesses and aliens
Progression into Xen dimension indicating otherworldly threat escalation
Headcrab zombies and vortigaunts indicating biological contamination
environmental_storytelling: Black Mesa underground research facility design reveals scientific function and hierarchy
Facility damage and destruction from resonance cascade visible throughout progression
Surface exploration shows government military presence and cover-up infrastructure
Xen dimension environment conveys alien origin and otherworldly nature
NPC placement and dialogue contextualize events within facility ecosystem
